types.evidence: Hosted the clia-sprite modularization session as the carrier-shape harness: parsed the operator's modularization brief, kept the Claude agent persona aligned to the reshape goal across two phases (engine kits first, sprite app split second), and routed all build/test/launch operations through Bash + Edit + Write tools without breaking the workspace contract.

Maintained working-tree continuity across ~25 file writes, three swift-build cycles, two xcodebuild target rebuilds, and four trash-and-relaunch hardware verification cycles. No working state lost; collective-level `.gitignore` confirmed correct so build outputs never staged.

Surfaced and recovered from a JSON-edit anchor failure during winddown — first chronicle Edit landed in `notes` array because multiple top-level arrays share closing-bracket shape; recovered via two-edit shift (insert into `entries`, remove from `notes`). Lesson preserved as 7th detail bullet on the clia entry.

details: Carrier-side observation: the SPM kit + xcodebuild + open + log-show iteration loop is the right tempo for hardware-on-the-table verification. Each cycle was ~30-90 seconds (build + relaunch + log slice), and the unified-log predicate `processImagePath CONTAINS "CLIA Sprite"` is the single most useful diagnostic anchor for ObjC++ engines reporting via NSLog.

Phantom-process recovery (LaunchServices keeping zombie processes alive when build artifacts share bundle ids) required `pkill -9` rather than the default SIGTERM. macOS Sequoia removed `lsregister -kill` so the database can no longer be force-cleared; the only working pattern is to trash the offending `.app` and let LaunchServices reconcile naturally.

TCC consent flow under bundle-id changes is asymmetric: the mac app's `me.rismay.clia.sprite` bundle id was unchanged from the prior single-target shape, so Camera/Microphone/Speech consent persisted across the reshape. The helper's new `me.rismay.clia.sprite.helper` bundle id needed fresh consent prompts. Worth flagging when planning bundle-id renames as part of refactors.
entries.summary: Carrier-side log of the clia-sprite modularization session. Hulk hosted the Claude persona through two phases (engine kits + app reshape) plus hardware verification on the BRIO. Three pattern lessons logged: SPM iteration tempo, LaunchServices phantom-process recovery, TCC consent under bundle-id renames.
entries.title: Winddown - hosting clia-sprite modularization (carrier view)
